case_scope: Canonical concentric-tube double-pipe heat exchanger with both fluids in co-current arrangement, single-phase liquid on both sides (no phase change), fixed geometry (no iterative sizing), heat balance closure at design point. Upstream paths exercised: TASK-007 (tube + annulus single-phase correlations) + TASK-006 (heat balance solver) + TASK-008 (fixed-geometry double-pipe rating). Pressure drop is explicitly NOT_COMPUTABLE (TASK-020+ scope; excluded from TASK-019 by frozen design §6).
amendment_002e_scope: TASK-019-GOLDEN-01 non-transitional operating point and expected_output re-freeze
amendment_basis: TASK-019 case_01 design-amendment history (audit-grounded). Amendment 001 (PR branch codex/task-019-freeze-validation-vectors) originally froze the canonical case input vectors, the canonical expected output vectors for the TASK-006/007/008-authorized fields, and the per-field tolerance values for case 01 using engineering-literature-referenced canonical baselines (Kern, Process Heat Transfer, 1950 — water-water 1"/2" tube-in-shell reference); no production calculation was executed in that round. Amendment 002-D introduced provider-canonical fluid identifiers (Water / HEOS via CoolProp) and is preserved in full. Amendment 002-E (this amendment, 2026-07-08, PR branch docs/task-019-amendment-002e-case01-nontransitional-vectors) SUPERSEDES only the following eight case_01 fields with production-chain-derived non-transitional vectors computed by the existing TASK-006 / TASK-007 / TASK-008 / TASK-015A production chain using CoolProp v8.0.0 at hot_mass_flow_kg_s = 0.75 and cold_mass_flow_kg_s = 0.75 (tube-side Re = 75810.66, annulus-side Re = 11386.37, both outside the TASK-007 transitional regime 2300 <= Re <= 10000): input.cold_side.mass_flow_kg_s, input.hot_side.mass_flow_kg_s, expected_output.heat_duty_W, expected_output.LMTD_derived_values.LMTD_counterflow_K, expected_output.heat_transfer_coefficients.annulus_side_W_m2_K, expected_output.heat_transfer_coefficients.tube_side_W_m2_K, expected_output.outlet_temperatures_K.cold_side, expected_output.outlet_temperatures_K.hot_side. The following case_01 properties are PRESERVED UNCHANGED by Amendment 002-E: input.cold_side.fluid_composition, input.hot_side.fluid_composition, input.cold_side.fluid_identifier (Water / HEOS), input.hot_side.fluid_identifier (Water / HEOS), input.cold_side.inlet_pressure_Pa, input.hot_side.inlet_pressure_Pa, input.cold_side.inlet_temperature_K, input.hot_side.inlet_temperature_K, input.geometry.* (all geometry fields), input.fouling_factors.*, input.property_provider_id, pressure_drop_excluded_from_taska_019 (still NOT_COMPUTABLE / TASK-020+ excluded), and the per-field numeric tolerance values for case_01 (the Amendment 001 tolerance basis — deterministic numerical behavior of the TASK-006/007/008 frozen upstream contracts and the CoolProp property provider's documented numerical accuracy at the operating envelope — is preserved; only the per-field basis notes were updated to reference Amendment 002-E, see _tolerance_metadata.json). case_02 (materials / mass / mechanical) and case_03 (cost / lifecycle envelope) are NOT superseded by Amendment 002-E. Pressure-drop remains NOT_COMPUTABLE (TASK-020+ excluded). Provenance is recorded in _provenance_metadata.json. The six case_01 expected_output central values in this fixture are production-chain-derived, not engineering-literature baselines; the Amendment 001 wording that 'all numeric vectors are engineering-literature-referenced canonical baselines' applies only to the case_01 properties preserved unchanged above (and to case_02 / case_03).

geometry.shell_material_id: SS304 (stainless steel 304, per TASK-017 approved material catalog)
geometry.tube_material_id: SS304 (stainless steel 304, per TASK-017 approved material catalog)

slice3a_blocked_field_paths.heat_duty_W_pending: TBD-by-Slice-3A (will be computed by Slice 3A adapter from rate_double_pipe(HeatBalanceResult.heat_duty_W))
slice3a_blocked_field_paths.LMTD_derived_values.LMTD_counterflow_K_p: TBD-by-Slice-3A (will be computed by Slice 3A adapter from rate_double_pipe(HeatBalanceResult.LMTD_K))
slice3a_blocked_field_paths.heat_transfer_coefficients.annulus_side_: TBD-by-Slice-3A (will be computed by Slice 3A adapter from rate_double_pipe(HeatBalanceResult.annulus_h))
slice3a_blocked_field_paths.heat_transfer_coefficients.tube_side_W_m: TBD-by-Slice-3A (will be computed by Slice 3A adapter from rate_double_pipe(HeatBalanceResult.tube_h))
slice3a_blocked_field_paths.outlet_temperatures_K.cold_side_pending: TBD-by-Slice-3A (will be computed by Slice 3A adapter from rate_double_pipe(HeatBalanceResult.outlet_T_cold))
slice3a_blocked_field_paths.outlet_temperatures_K.hot_side_pending: TBD-by-Slice-3A (will be computed by Slice 3A adapter from rate_double_pipe(HeatBalanceResult.outlet_T_hot))
slice3a_blocked_field_paths.slice3a_blocked_field_paths_note: Each TBD-by-Slice-3A marker below is the audit-trail record of an authorized TASK-019 expected_output field path that would have been computed by the upstream TASK-006/007/008 chain had the Slice 3A adapter been wired up. The marker field names use the <real_path_leaf>_pending convention so the audit trail maps 1-to-1 to the real authorized field paths above; the marker values carry the Slice 3A production-source symbol that the future Slice 3A adapter will project into the field. The Slice 2 _materialize_not_computable_actual_output walks the expected_output subtree for TBD-by-Slice-N markers and surfaces them as actual_output.blocked_fields per the P0-1 contract. pressure-drop is NOT enumerated here (TASK-020+ excluded; surfaced separately as a pressure-drop per_field record). discount/salvage are NOT enumerated here (TASK-018 §5.3 / §5.3.2 deferred; preserved verbatim in case_03).

amendment_1_skeleton_note: Slice 1 fixture-shape reproducibility was preserved (case_scope / case_title / schema_version / tolerance_profile_id / provenance_profile_id / license_boundary_attestation / pressure_drop_excluded_from_taska_019). All TBD-by-Slice-2 input and expected_output placeholders were replaced with frozen canonical numeric vectors per the TASK-019 design-amendment round 2026-07-08. Pressure drop remains NOT_COMPUTABLE. No TASK-020+ field was introduced. The expected_output subtree additionally carries a sibling slice3a_blocked_field_paths block: each TBD-by-Slice-3A marker is the P0-1-contract audit-trail record of an authorized TASK-019 expected_output field path that would have been computed had the Slice 3A adapter been wired up; the marker field names use the <real_path_leaf>_pending convention so the audit trail maps 1-to-1 to the frozen numeric fields above without overwriting them.
